response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through founding GISCollective and building web GIS applications, plus real low-latency systems experience writing asynchronous DLang services handling millions of requests per second. However, their modern C++ experience is brief and dated (a ~6-month stint in 2014-2015), they have no Rust, and their recent decade of work centers on web/full-stack TypeScript rather than systems-level pipeline engineering. There is no evidence of routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline work, so while the systems aptitude and geo interest are promising, the core skill match for this role is weak.
strengths: Founder of an open-source geospatial data platform (GISCollective) with sustained multi-year ownership
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, and testing tool authorship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles are web/full-stack product work, not distributed data pipelines or systems engineering

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain and low-latency systems experience
